title: Segmentation fault in CROSS JOIN and the rtree extension
description:
mrigger added on 2020-10-01 11:35:08:
The following test case triggers a segmentation fault in latest development version of SQLite ([4a43430fd2]).

CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE rt0 USING rtree(c0, c1, c2);
CREATE VIEW v0(c0) AS SELECT 1;
INSERT INTO rt0(c1) VALUES (0);
SELECT * FROM v0 CROSS JOIN rt0 ON rt0.c1 IN (SELECT 1) WHERE rt0.c1>0; -- Segmentation fault
drh added on 2020-10-01 13:33:28:
Bisect to the IN-early-out optimization improvements of 
check-in [35505c68c1945c35].  The test script does not fail
in any released version of SQLite, as far as I can tell at
this point.  Investigation is ongoing.
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE rt1 (id INT PRIMARY KEY AUTO_INCREMENT, minX FLOAT, minY FLOAT);
CREATE VIEW v1 AS SELECT 2 AS id;
SELECT * FROM v1 CROSS JOIN rt1 WHERE rt1.minX = 2 AND rt1.minY > 0;
key_bug_features: CROSS JOIN with rtree extension causes segmentation fault
